actual_coverage.reasoning: Both ask for the multi-year, structural drivers behind growth (Perfecta, yield/cost/other levers) relative to today vs pre-pandemic.
Direct quarterly yield cadence question; predicted asks for the quarterly trajectory behind the full-year guide including the Q2 headwind.
Both target Mediterranean Q3: remaining inventory, size of the booking slowdown, and the assumptions embedded in forward guidance.
Focus on booking trajectory vs earlier in the year and whether demand is close-in vs forward, including visibility into 2027.
Both ask for yield/APD uplift and ramp cadence from Beach Club/Perfect Day (including 2027–2028 timing) as the Western Caribbean builds.
Actual asks construction status/resumption in Mexico; predicted covers economics/ramp of destination assets, not construction.
Actual asks whether 2024/2025 Europe headwinds reverse in 2027; predicted tests sensitivity of multi-year targets to prolonged Med weakness.
Both decompose yield by drivers (new hardware, private destinations vs like-for-like) and touch on regional Europe expectations.
Actual is about itinerary changes due to higher fuel and any 2027 deferrals; no prediction targets itinerary shifts or deferrals.
Both address hedging stance in a high fuel environment and EPS sensitivity to fuel movements.
Both probe how loyalty/repeat and demographic shifts affect booking lead times and spend, and thus yield.
Actual focuses on North American airfare effects; predicted focuses on Med/Europe disruptions including air costs—adjacent but different region and ask.

missed_actual_themes: Construction/status update for Perfect Day Mexico (pause and resumption)
Specific 2027 recapture of 2024/2025 European headwinds (carryover vs rebound)
Itinerary changes due to higher fuel and any 2027 deferrals vs harvesting efficiencies
Impact of higher North American airfares on NA customer booking behavior
overpredicted_themes: Detailed ship-level economics for Icon/Legend and orderbook optionality
CapEx/FCF cadence and balance sheet refinancing specifics
Deep pre-cruise/onboard category mix and AI testing detail beyond loyalty effects
summary: Coverage was solid on booking trajectory (Med slowdown and rebound), quarterly yield cadence, yield decomposition (hardware/islands vs like-for-like), private destination ramp timing, hedging approach/sensitivity, and loyalty/repeat guest impacts. Gaps were on construction status of Perfect Day Mexico, itinerary changes due to fuel and any spend deferrals, explicit 2027 recapture of Europe headwinds, and North America airfare effects. Precision was moderate; many predictions focused on capital allocation, JV details, fleet/orderbook, and CapEx topics that did not appear in the actual Q&A.
